notes: SafeTravelPH (SafeTravel PH Mobility Innovations Organization, Inc.) is a UP Diliman-affiliated crowdsourcing platform. Its open data inventory primarily contains operational telemetry (GPS tracks, boarding/alighting) rather than route databases. The EDSA Busway dataset (2023) is the only NCR dataset; it includes vehicle GPS tracks and stop coordinates but no GTFS file. Stop list and fare data below compiled from public sources (TopGear PH, Philippine Beaches, edsacarousel.ph). SafeTravelPH data inventory: https://www.safetravelph.org/data-inventory (ODbLv1 license).

routes.frequency_notes: 24/7 service; reduced frequency midnight-4 AM; peak frequency not specified in source
routes.notes: 24 southbound stops. Fare is distance-based, rounded to nearest PHP 0.25. 20% discount for students, seniors, PWDs (min fare PHP 12). GCash accepted as of December 2025. GPS coordinates of stops exist in SafeTravelPH 2023 EDSA Busway dataset but not publicly downloadable as standalone file. Route designation: Route 1. Operated under DOTr/LTFRB. Most stops on EDSA median islands. Rail interchanges at Balintawak (LRT-1), Fernando Poe Jr./Roosevelt (LRT-1), North Avenue (MRT-3), Kamuning (MRT-3), Santolan-Annapolis (MRT-3), Ortigas (MRT-3), Guadalupe (MRT-3), Buendia (MRT-3), One Ayala (MRT-3), Taft Avenue (MRT-3), PITX (LRT-1).

routes.frequency_notes: 24/7 service; reduced frequency midnight-4 AM; peak frequency not specified in source
routes.notes: 23 northbound stops (one fewer than southbound: Tramo stop not served northbound; City of Dreams stop not served southbound). Asymmetric routing around Ayala/Makati area. Same fare structure as southbound. Same operator and payment methods. SafeTravelPH EDSA Busway 2023 dataset contains GPS tracks and stop coordinates for this route.
